persistent_components.description: number of connected components whose death epsilon exceeds 4.182 (plus 1 surviving)

persistent_loops.interpretation: peak beta_1 across filtration — upper bound on independent cycles in 1-skeleton interpretable as healing-loop obstructions in feature space

interpretation: Vietoris-Rips filtration on the 449-row standardized 78-dim preflight feature matrix. beta_0 starts at 449 (each point isolated) and falls gradually as epsilon grows; beta_1 (cycle count via Euler characteristic on the 1-skeleton) grows rapidly past epsilon~2, indicating that preflight outputs span a highly NON-SIMPLY-CONNECTED region of feature space. Using q75 of the death-epsilon distribution (4.18) as a lifetime threshold, 112 components persist past that scale, suggesting O(113) distinguishable memory-state regimes at fine granularity. At the median pairwise distance epsilon~10.0 the complex has consolidated to beta_0=7 components — consistent with a small number of coarse attractors, on the same order as the 4 recommended_action classes (USE_MEMORY / WARN / ASK_USER / BLOCK).
notes: Uses union-find for beta_0 (connected components) and Euler characteristic beta_1 = E - V + beta_0 on the 1-skeleton (an UPPER bound on the true H_1 rank once 2-simplices are included).
beta_2 requires enumerating 3-simplices: O(n^3) combinatorial cost in 77D — skipped per task spec.
Feature matrix derived from 449 live /v1/preflight runs on the benchmark corpus. Non-synthetic: real scoring engine output.
